creator: wayne@crinc.com
text:
Folks this has been solved!  Someone responded and suggested a blocksize issue.  We always set the blocksize to 0 and let the drive auto handle blocksize.  We have been assuming that out-of-the-box tape drives default to blocksize 0.  Either BSD messed with it or it was not defaulted to 0. Issuing the command mt blocksize 0 solved the write problem.

Many thanks for everyones assistance with this!  I would have ordered the parts for a scsi tape on Monday.

Wayne
